title: How to Meditate on God? | Bhagavad Gita Chapter 10 Verses 17 - 21 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So, today we will discuss from the 10th chapter of the Gita, the topic is how to meditate
on God, based on the teachings of Srila Prabhupada.
Let's chant this verse together, this is the 17th verse.
Katham vidyam aham yogims, tuam sada pari chintayan,
keshu keshu cha bhaveshu, chintyo si bhagvan mayan,
How should I meditate on you?
In what various forms are you to be contemplated, O blessed Lord?
So these are the words spoken by Arjuna to Krishna.
He is saying, how do I meditate on you?
What are the various forms in which we can think of you, we can contemplate on you?
Now why Arjuna is asking this question?
As far as he being the devotee is concerned, for him to remember the Lord, to think of
In fact, he has the Lord in his company.
He has the association of the Lord, Lord is right there with him.
He is asking this question so that people like us, who are not able to think of Lord,
who are not able to see the Lord.
So that people can get some clue how we can contemplate on the Lord.
Now Krishna in the Gita says, naham prakasha sarvasya, naham means I do not, prakasha means
reveal, become evident, become visible, prakasha sarvasya, sarvasya means to everyone, I do
not reveal myself, I am not evident to, I am not visible to, sarvasya, sarvasya means
Yogo maya samanvrta, I am covered by my yoga maya potency.
Although the Lord is there, Prahlad Maharaj was asked, is the Lord there in the pillar?
He very confidently said, yes he is there.
But Hrindakashuku could not see that Lord.
So those who are rebellious, who are, that's why the word used is saintly kings understood
Unless we become saintly, we are not able to perceive the Lord.
Bhaktia mam abhijanati yavan yas chasmi tatvata.
Only through love, only through devotion, bhaktia mam abhijanati, can I be known.
Although the Lord is omnipresent, he is present everywhere, but we are not able to see it.
The example is given just like the butter is there in the milk, but it is not visible.
But by following a process, the butter manifests.
So by culturing devotion, by becoming a devotee of the Lord, by surrendering to the Lord,
by following the process, we can also perceive the Lord, the Lord becomes manifest.
Now for devotees who don't have yoga, maya, samanvrta, who can see the Lord always, they
to contemplate on. The Lord is evident. They can perceive the presence of Lord every moment.
They can experience the reciprocation of the Lord. But what about people like us?
We are not able to see the Lord. Our faith is very Komala. We are not very sure Lord exists,
not exists. Many incidences we see in this world around us which bewilders us.
So, maya has two forms. All of us have heard about this word maya? Yes, no? Yes. At least
you must have heard that maya has two forms. When the light falls on a mirror,
it gets reflected. So, one side of the mirror where the light falls and it gets reflected,
it is illuminated. It has light. And that place is called Abhasa. Abhasa means
that potency of the Lord by which everything can be seen in relation with the Lord
can be perceived. We can perceive the presence of Lord in everything and in everyone.
There is another potency called Mahamaya which is Tamaha, Tamaha means Darkness.
The below bottom side of the mirror in which the light falls and gets reflected is Dark.
And what is the darkness when we are not able to perceive the Lord, when we see things independent of Lord, when Lord is not in the picture?
So devotees of the Lord are under the influence of Yogamaya.
Yashoda Mai could not perceive that Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead. She was covered by the Yogamaya potency of the Lord.
We who have forgotten the Lord, rebelled against the Lord, are under the influence of Mahamaya, which is darkness.
Krishna Surya Sama Maya Haya Amaya
The Lord Krishna is like Surya. Surya means sun. Maya Haya Andhakaar. Maya is like darkness.
Wherever there is sun, there cannot be any darkness.
So likewise, when we bring the Lord into our life, the remembrance of the Lord in our life,
when we are able to see things in relationship with the Supreme Lord, Maya has no grip on us.
Why we say we are trapped in the web of Maya, because we are doing activities completely oblivious of the Lord and His pleasure, His satisfaction.
The goal of life has become my pleasure, my satisfaction.
Could be extended pleasure, my family's pleasure, my society's pleasure, my country's pleasure.
So that is Tamaha, that is darkness.
And that is why it says Tamaseema Jyotirgamaya.
It is said Brahma Satyam Jagat Mithya.
This is Shankaracharya who said this Brahman is Satya and this Jagat is Mithya.
This world which we are living in is false.
A question to all of you, this world is false, yes or no?
All what we see around us, FIFA World Cup happening, it is all illusion or it is a reality?
How many of you say it is a reality? FIFA World Cup is actually happening.
But here it is saying it is Mithya, it is illusion.
Now how many of us say that this is a snake?
How many of us say it is not a snake, it is a rope?
How many of us say it is both?
If we perceive a rope as a snake, that is illusion.
If you see a rope and it looks like a snake because of something, that is illusion.
Rope is not Mithya, rope is not unreal.
But perception of rope as snake is Mithya.
So when we perceive Jagat, independent of the Lord, with no relation with the Lord, that is Mithya.
But Jagat per se is not Mithya.
But that understanding that there is no connection with the Lord, there is no Lord, I am the Lord, that is Mithya.
One time, this is a twist to the story which some of you would have heard about, Honest Thieves.
Please very carefully hear this story.
You will be able to relate with what is happening in this world.
One time there were 20, group of 20 people, out of those 24 were ladies, women and 2 children.
They all got together and they decided to rob a bank.
Each one had some very specific role to play, including the children, in robbing the bank.
So they all schemed together, planned together and they very meticulously executed the plan.
And they finally succeeded in robbing the bank.
And they fled with the booty, with all the cash in the bags.
And finally reached a place, a hideout which they had initially planned.
And then they all got together and said, now let's distribute the wealth.
Which we have jointly, so to say, robbed.
So 4 of them were very, you know, well built, muscular, you know, huge bodied men.
They said, no, no, we did the main thing.
We actually went and, you know, broke the lockers.
So most of the wealth should come to us.
So in that process, in that discussion of how to divide the wealth, they started.
Who will get what percentage of the share.
So while this fight was on, they almost fought like cats and dogs.
So one of them was relatively elder in the group.
He said, let's not fight like cats and dogs.
I don't think we'll come to consensus like this.
And all of us are having heated arguments.
I don't think today we'll be able to resolve.
Let's convene a meeting tomorrow morning.
And with fresh mind, let's think how to divide this wealth.
So next day morning they assembled.
And then one of them said, I think if we just argue like that, we are not going to reach any consensus.
Let's use a democratic process to divide this wealth.
And that person will be empowered to decide how the wealth will be divided.
So while the election process was on,
So now the question came, should the women vote or not?
Should these children be given voting rights or not?
It became a women rights issue.
The children should be voted on.
Then finally one of them suggested, again looks like we are not going to reach consensus.
Let's have a four man committee.
And let this committee come up with a constitution.
So again they started jointly discussing and all those things.
Mind you they are talking about big big words of democratic way.
Nice way of dividing so that it is fair.
But what are they talking about?
What wealth they are talking about?
In the meantime the police came and they caught hold of them and they were taken to police custody.
And they were brought to the trial court.
In the trial court again the advocate who was representing this case he said,
My lord although they have robbed the bank but please understand the spirit.
They were trying to distribute the wealth very justfully, very rightfully.
You know they were having a consensus.
They were democratic way of this thing.
After all it is a robbery.
Now in this world we have democratic way of electing government.
We have a constitution which we abide by.
We try to assure that everyone there is justice.
But if we don't understand a very fundamental truth.
That this property what we have.
Then Prabhupada used this word sarcastically.
If we don't recognize that fundamental thing.
All the abilities what I have.
It all is coming from the lord.
Today I may be having this house.
I may have a document signed that this house belongs to me.
The minerals whatever that has gone in building the house.
Before I came in it was existing there.
And after I depart I will still exist.
So all these terms just like this honest thieves.
They were very honestly trying to very justifiably distribute the wealth.
But unless we recognize that this wealth is not mine.
But we are at best called as honest thieves.
Because the materialists who are not able to see the lord.
Cannot see the hand of the lord.
Cannot able to see the relationship with the lord.
On which we can meditate on.
In which we can perceive the presence of the lord.
Of your mighty potencies and glories.
For I never tire of hearing your ambrosial words.
Please tell me your mighty glories and potencies.
No matter how much you speak about your glories.
So to say of hearing them.

Very keen on hearing the glories and potencies of the lord.
A pure devotee of the lord.
Hearing more and more about the lord.

What are we interested in hearing.
What is Virat Kohli and Aishika doing.
A pure devotee of the lord.
Problem is not with the sugar candy.
The problem is with his disease.
When we come out of the disease state.
When we come out of Mahamaya.
Just like a person who recovers from jaundice.
He will see the sugar candy.

Please give me that valuable stone.
Please give me that valuable stone.

He put his hand in his jholi.
And moment he opened his hand.
What stone is he talking about?
He rushed back to his home.
I am going to be the richest man.
I am going to be the happiest man.
He didn't discuss with his family.
He was tossing and turning in the bed.

He could not sleep in the night.
What is the use of this wealth?
I am not able to sleep.
He rushed back to the same sadhu.
And the sadhu was busy chanting the names of the lord.
He went back to that sadhu.
I have come back to return this.
Happy that you got this stone.
What wealth he is talking about.
and again and enjoy it for years together. There are people chanting the

Once we cross that threshold level, just like the rockets when they lift
There is a threshold velocity. Initially the amount of fuel which it consumes is
phenomenal. Once it gets into the orbit it
seamlessly moves. So after we cross that threshold this thing it is Kevala Ananda

that. We are aspiring for that. We are hoping against hope that this will happen,
we will get this, I will be happy. But what we are actually striving for is
Kevala Ananda Khanda. Unadulterated bliss and the path for that is what our
scriptures is talking about. In fact scriptures are not aiming gunning for
something which, you know, which we don't want. They are asking us or rather
showing us a path to achieve the same thing. We are just waiting for that to come.
what we are wanting to achieve. Just that we have chosen a different path, a wrong
path. A path which appears lucrative but will lead in a wrong direction. Now in
reply to the questions asked by Arjuna, Krishna says, Shri Bhagwan Uvacha, the
Blessed Lord said, yes I will tell you of my splendorous manifestations but only
of those which are prominent. Oh Arjuna, for my opulence is limitless. Now Krishna
is implying that yes you have asked me what manifestation we can contemplate on
you. He says, yes I will explain my glories, my splendorous manifestations
but only those which are prominent. For my opulences are limitless, kya kya main
bolu, kya kya, what all, everything is emanating from me, sarvasya prabhavo matta sarvam
pravartate, everything is emanating from me. It's not possible to comprehend the
greatness of Krishna and all his opulences. It's not possible. Therefore
going to share with you some principal details. Just like for example when we
have to prepare for exams, we have limited time assuming two hours, then what
we do, we bullet point some important highlights in front of the chapters
there, some this thing, depending, time is less. So likewise, now Arjuna is saying,
explain to me your glorious manifestation. Krishna says, it's
limitless, I will explain to you some principal important manifestation.
Now Krishna is describing what can be directly perceived by a common man.
I am the Self, O Gudakesha. Can anyone tell me what's the meaning of Gudakesha?
Gudakesha means one who has conquered sleep. We all are tired of sleep. We all are feeling
Conquered by? Sleep. So there is a difference, right? We are also Gudakesha, but there is a difference between Mahamaya and Yogamaya.
Both have Maya. So we are also one form of Gudakesha, but rather than conquering sleep, we are conquered by sleep.
I am the Self, O Gudakesha, seated in the hearts of all creatures.
Sometimes when we open a book, sometimes a very old book, have you seen one small, miniscule, some insect kind of a thing walking?
Very small, it is almost like a full stop on that book.
It is a living entity. The moment you put your finger on it, it is a living entity.
Whether it will turn the way, yes or no? It is conscious.
It has intelligence. It has some emotions. It can sense danger.
Matter cannot move on its own. When a person dies, the body is there, but there is no movement.
So Krishna is saying, I am there in every living entity.
When a person becomes a devotee, automatically, he doesn't have to, he will automatically become compassionate.
He sees the presence of Lord everywhere.
How can he kill an animal and eat for his taste buds?
Just because that creature is lowly, for example in a family, there are three children, one of them is disabled, cannot walk.
Can we take advantage of his disability?
Because it is not artificial, it is not sentimental stuff.
He sees the presence of the Lord.
I am the beginning, the middle and end of all beings.
When there was nothing, I was there.
Creation has come about, stays for some time and will get annihilated.
When there was nothing, the Lord was there.
It is being maintained by the Lord.
And when everything will be annihilated, still the Lord remains.
Arjuna is addressed as Gudakesha, which means one who has conquered the darkness of sleep.
Sleep is very important, we cannot ignore it.
That's why Krishna in the Gita says, one who eats too much or does not eat enough.
Sleeps too much or does not sleep enough cannot be a yogi.
One who is regulated in his eating, sleeping, recreation and work
can mitigate all material pains arising from material contact.
So sleep, with the understanding that yes, body needs some rest,
we don't become attached to sleep.
We don't reach a stage where we enjoy it.
Yes, the body needs whatever minimum is required for the body to work properly.
We do that and do not waste unnecessarily time in sleeping.
For many, sleep becomes a source of enjoyment.
We have a lot of things to do.
So rather than be conquered by the sleep, Gudakesha is one who conquers the darkness of sleep.
And by the way, we have an assumption that yes, average man has to sleep for 7 hours, 8 hours.
As we become spiritually advanced, the bodily necessities automatically diminishes.
Prabhupada was fit, travelling all over the world,
He was sleeping 3 or 4 hours whole day.
Very enthusiastic, speaking to the reporters, conversing with the devotees, having meetings,
writing books, travelling, answering to the letters,
because they are on a spiritual platform.
The Lord is manifest as Super Soul in each and every living entity.
One time, Akbar and Birbal, they were together.
And Akbar told Birbal that sometimes I am amused by what is mentioned in your Hindu scriptures.
It is mentioned that the Lord personally comes.
He is with the devotees. He is there in the heart as Super Soul.
This all is absurd. How can the Lord personally be there as Paramatma as Super Soul with everyone?
How do I reconcile all this absurd stuff?
So Birbal didn't reply to what he says.
Give me some time, I will explain.
Few days later, Akbar and Birbal, they were having a walk on the side of a river.
And at a distance, they saw that there was a boat and which the nurse, one of the maid servant was having.
Having the daughter of Akbar on her lap and was giving a boat ride.
And as soon as she saw Akbar and Birbal approaching,
she got up with the idea of doing Namaskar.
And in the process, unfortunately, the baby fell in the water.
And moment the Akbar saw that the baby has fallen in the water,
without considering, without remaining with his royal robes, he plunged into the water and started swimming and reached the spot where the baby had fallen.
And moment he reached that spot, he caught hold of that baby and he realized it was a doll.
He knew somebody has played with him.
And then behind him, Birbal plunged into the water and reached that same spot.
And Birbal told Akbar, now you understand.
Moment you saw your son or your daughter in water, you didn't mind plunging yourself.
You could have told me, you could have told your ministers.
But you plunged yourself. You didn't mind soiling your royal robes in this water and saving your child.
So do you think it is absurd for the Lord to personally come in this Samsara Sagara to save his own children?
And that is one supreme manifestation of the Lord.
He is present with each one of us as Paramahansa.
He is present with each one of us as Paramahansa.
Our scriptures say it is like two birds sitting on the same tree.
Have you heard of this analogy?
How many of us have heard about this?
Two birds sitting on the same tree. One bird is completely focused on eating the fruits.
Completely oblivious of the second bird.
And sometimes the fruits are sweet.
What does the second bird represent?

you will cross over all obstacles of conditional life.

Then Bhagavatam says, Atma Sainyashu Asat Sophi, this is Asatya.
It is not real protection. Atma Sainyashu Asat Sophi. Tesham Pramatta Nidhanam Pashyanapina Pashyate.
Pramatta means when a person is maddened, when a person is under the influence of Mahamaya.
Pramatta Nidhanam, the eventual thing that he is going to believe everything is going to die, nothing is going to protect him.
Nothing will come to his protection. He is not able to see.
Pashyanapina Pashyate, he is not able to see.
Unfortunately, just there is one person who attends our class. He is quite regular.
Just yesterday, he sent me a message. He says, Prabhaji, please pray for my son.
Some terrible infection has started. He is admitted in a hospital.
you will not be able to see it.
You know, mother on the WhatsApp, invariably in status, you know, you have son going to school with bag, smiling face.
And today, if you see that picture, you will not be able to see.
We have a false sense that now I am secure, now this is there, family is there.
That is why it says, Pramatta Nidhanam Pashyanapina Pashyate.
We are not able to see. Why we are not able to see?
Come out of the darkness, come to light.
